properties.entry: Ignition offers a range of querying options that offer a tremendous amount of power and flexibility. In addition to on-change querying, the system can perform advanced functions such as querying tag from multiple sources, calculate quality, interpolate their values and more.
This video shows a demo of linking Ignition's security to Auth0, a federated identity provider.

A demo project showcasing most of Ignition's features: realtime status & control, historical trending, UDTs & templates, reporting, alarming, security, and more. To login as an Administrator use admin/password as the username/password. To login as an operator use operator/password as the username/password.
Docked views allow you to have a fixed view locked to any side of the screen. Both the header and navigation in this demo project are examples of docked views.

Ignition has Quality built into Tags automatically. Data Quality is the measure of how reliable a particular Tag's data is. If a Tag's quality is not Good, the value generally should not be trusted, and an overlay is shown to make the Tag's bad quality visible.
Transaction Groups are an easy way to store data historically, load recipe values and synchronise database values to an OPC.

You can easily try out Ignition on your own mobile device. Just scan this QR code with your cell phone camera or favorite QR code reader to navigate to this Perspective Demo Project. Use the text box below to create your own QR code!

Many mobile devices have a built-in component called an accelerometer, which can be used to find the device's orientation. Tap the Accelerometer button and try it out for yourself. The bubble will update as you move your device around. Hold your device flat, and try to keep it level!
The video below shows how docked views respond to different screen sizes and user input.

Ignition can easily be configured to store alarm data into a SQL database, using the Alarm Journal. This Alarm Journal stores basic data about alarms that have occurred, such as their source, timestamp, associated data on the alarm, and the values of the alarm's properties at the time the event occurred.
The Ignition Perspective App, allows you to bring your data with you where ever you go.

Data retrieved from the database can also be displayed in a tabular format. Tables can include the raw data or can be filtered and sorted. The inventory table below can be filtered by Type or Location and rows can be deleted by simply clicking the trash icon embedded in the last cell.

An Ignition Onboard Device installed with Ignition Edge IIoT is connected to a MicroLogix PLC via ethernet. Edge IIoT is publishing tags from the MicroLogix to an MQTT broker in the cloud. This broker is an Ignition Gateway that is using the MQTT Distributor Module. The Ignition Gateway that is running this Online Demo project is using the MQTT Engine to subscribe to the same MQTT broker, with read/write access to the published tags. When you use the toggle buttons below, Ignition is sending a command back to the MQTT broker, which then sends it to the Ignition Edge IIoT device, ultimately writing the new values to the PLC.
Try adding or modifying a user and role in the User Management resource below.

With powerful, easy-to-use tools for building dynamic, database-driven PDF reports with pixel-perfect layout, Ignition is perfect for creating virtually any kind of industrial report, including: SCADA reporting, industrial project reports, production management reports, OEE and downtime tracking, SPC reports, QA reports, compliance reports, historical data analysis reports, and more.

The User Management Perspective Exchange resource provides the same functionality as Vision's User Management component. If the user source that is selected is from a database or is internal to Ignition, then it is possible to view, add, edit, and delete users using this resource.

entry.key: Icons can also be set to different colors.

properties.entry: Icons can also be set to different colors.

Most mobile devices are capable of Near Field Communication (NFC) and the Perspective app can utilize NFC communication protocols. Open this page on the Perspective app, click on the Scan NFC button, and scan a NFC capable device. Once a NFC device is scanned a popup will display the information captured from the device.
Dashboards can be created for quick and easy data at a glance. Ignition has powerful charts and tables and even supports making your own custom graphics using Ignition's built-in drawing toolkit.

Ignition makes working with HMIs easy, it installs in minutes on virtually any device, and updating an HMI with Ignition is fast and painless. With the included Ignition Designer, you can build HMIs with historical trending, alarming, and more with drag-and-drop ease.

Realtime and Historical tags can be selected from the folder tree and added to show the historical data on the Power Chart. The Perspective Power Chart allows for pan and zoom, x-trace, range brush, exporting to CSV and printing.
This video shows how style classes allow you to quickly make beautiful and consistent interface.
Ignition has built-in Map Support for OpenStreetMap and Google Maps. You can add your own pins, shapes, and layers.

entry.key: This button has no security requirement.

properties.entry: This button has no security requirement.

entry.key: How to Launch the Vision Demo

properties.entry: How to Launch the Vision Demo
In addition to polling on a schedule, Transaction Groups can be set to run by a trigger. Try it with the button below!

The Perspective Document Management Project is an Ignition Exchange resource that allows a simple, yet effect way to easily view and manage documents. This project utilizes a SQL database to upload, view, and manage documents and can easily be downloaded and imported into any Perspective project.

To visualize historical data, Ignition provides multiple chart options. These charts are configurable in the Ignition Designer as well as customizable in the client. Data to populate these charts can be pulled automatically from the Ignition Tag Historian or from any custom database query.

Preview complete Ignition applications across multiple industries. We've worked directly with systems integrators to develop these highly realistic products, giving you a glimpse into what a real Ignition project looks like. Launch any of the applications below on mobile or desktop. They're all mobile-friendly and cross platform compatible.

Using self-contained widgets, Go-Boards can be customized to display exactly what you need in a concise manner. In this example, you can see the current weather in 4 different locations across the United States, along with a news feed.
Modify, insert, and control using our form components. Click on the date picker to see a full calendar popup.

entry.key: 1. Download and install the Vision Client Launcher for your system:

properties.entry: 1. Download and install the Vision Client Launcher for you system:

There are an array of embedding components to choose from that exhibit different behaviors such as being able to collapse, rotate, duplicate as well as animate views, to name a few. Giving you more ways to customize the visualization of your data and create more meaningful and impactful projects.

entry.key: Change duration of cell Right Side

properties.entry: Change duration of cell Right Side

entry.key: <html>Ignition has built-in alarming functionality to see the current status of alarms, store alarms in a SQL database, and notify certain people when alarms occur. The page allows you to simulate alarms by changing the values of memory tags. Try changing the values below to see the alarms work.

properties.entry: <html>Ignition has built-in alarming functionality to see the current status of alarms, store alarms in a SQL database, and notify certain people when alarms occur. The page allows you to simulate alarms by changing the values of memory tags. Try changing the values below to see the alarms work.

For trending and other time-stamped data, time series charts can be used to display raw and aggregated data. The chart below can toggle between real-time and historical mode to specify the range of data you want to view.

entry.key: 6. Find the Vision Demo and select Launch Windowed:

properties.entry: 6. Find the Vision Demo and select Launch Windowed:

entry.key: Oil & Gas Demo - Remote Site Monitoring

properties.entry: Oil & Gas Demo - Remote Site Monitoring
Welcome to our Ignition demo application. This demo showcases most of Ignition's features. Please click on a feature below to learn more.

To give more control on how information is visualized, embedding components allow for multiple views to be displayed on a single page in various configurations. Embedding components as well as the embedded views within the component are mobile responsive, so your project can be automatically optimized for whatever device you are viewing on. To see this in action, view this demo on a variety of devices and see how it automatically adjusts.
Once the data is in the database, it can be queried to show summary and analysis information. Ignition can then display this data in charts, graphs, or text.
Watch out for the following overlays to quickly identify the quality of your data.
The charts in Ignition allow you to visualize your data in a meaningful way. Try hovering over the XY Chart or clicking on the Pie Chart to interact with them.

The table component can show historical data in a tabular format. Ignition offers a range of querying options that offer a tremendous amount of power and flexibility. In addition to on-change querying, the system can perform advanced functions such as querying tag from multiple sources, calculate quality, interpolate their values and more.

Two Factor Authentication allows Ignition to verify the identity of a user by requiring a second challenge after the standard username and password. One example of this would be requesting a code send to a registered mobile device.
The map below is interactive. Try "pinch and zoom" or "click and drag" to have a look around.

Perspective components have a Style property which allow you to customize the look and feel of components on the screen through various style options. A style can also be created and placed in the Styles folder, creating a Style Class. A Style Class allows you to define style elements in one place, and then quickly add that style to different components as you see fit.

entry.key: We do not keep a record of signatures submitted.

properties.entry: We do not keep a record of signatures submitted.

The sparkline chart is a minimalistic chart component that displays a line-chart history for a single data point. Sparklines are intended to show a great deal of contextual information in a very small amount of space. Sparklines are typically used to display the recent history (up to current time) of a data point so that the viewer can quickly discern the recent trend of a data point.

entry.key: Back Parking Sensors and Backup Camera

properties.entry: Back Parking Sensors and Backup Camera

The File Upload component is a quick and effortless way to gather files from users. On mobile devices this component doubles as access to the device’s camera, allowing you to share photos from the field.

The example reports below are generated by Ignition in real time. Reports in Ignition can be static, using the specific values and ranges set in the designer, or dynamic, allowing the user to select the data they want to see in the report. You can also make PDF barcodes that you can print using Zebra or other label maker printers. You can even use an existing PDF as the background of your report, with data placed on top of it, as shown in the Compliance Report.
The stack lights are currently unavailable. Please check back at a later time.
Check out the video below to see how Ignition was built from the ground up to be responsive.
Try adding new folders and files using the Document Management resource below!
User input components such as dropdowns and text areas allow for easy form generation. The following form allows you to insert data into our Inventory Table.
In addition to running on any device with a modern web browser, Ignition features dedicated apps for both IOS and Android. These apps allow for access to device hardware such as GPS and Cameras.
These components can be used to display data from machines, databases, or just static text.

Ignition has a built-in library of images for you to get started on your project with. If you have the Symbol Factory module installed, you have nearly 4,000 industrial high quality vector graphics symbols at your fingertips to use in your projects. You can add these images to your project as they are, or edit them to be exactly what you need. If you already have your own SVGs, they can be imported and used within Ignition as well.

There are a variety of container types that support different layout strategies. The layout strategy defines how the container displays each of its child components in the view. Try resizing your screen to see how each of the containers featured below reacts differently.

entry.key: 5. Select Import Application Config and import the VisionDemo.json file:

properties.entry: 5. Select Import Application Config and import the VisionDemo.json file:

Many mobile devices have a built-in component called an accelerometer, which can be used to find the device's orientation. Open up this page in the Perspective App, and then tap the Accelerometer button to try it out for yourself. The bubble will update as you move your device around. Hold your device flat, and try to keep it level!
Select and tour Ignition's powerful features

entry.key: Change duration of cell Left Side

properties.entry: Change duration of cell Left Side

Geolocation takes advantage of device-specific location services to help provide a location for a particular user at any given point in time. There are many common use cases for using geolocation in mobile apps, such as displaying weather data for a user’s location, navigating a user to a destination, or securing application use by location. Click on the enable GPS toggle and make sure to give permission to the Perspective native app.

The Perspective native app makes it easy to use the mobile device's camera to scan barcodes. Simply click on the scan barcode button and find a QR code to scan. The results of each scan will show in the text area.

entry.key: Try It In Our Reporting Example!

properties.entry: Try It In Our Reporting Example!
Icons can also be set to different colors.

High Performance HMI techniques and practices call for designs and displays which help the viewer make the best decision in the shortest amount of time after interacting with the HMI. To accomplish this, High Performance HMIs will often look basic and simplistic. They typically use gray-scale colors rather than the traditional graphics and bright colors for their displays. Conceptually, the High Performance HMI operates under the idea of visually contrasting critical and non-critical states. The power of this design philosophy is when something does go wrong, a High Performance HMI will quickly guide the user to the source of the problem.

Auditing is built-in to Ignition. You can simply turn it on and see everything that users do in the runtime from when they login to when they log out. This includes tag writes, reports generated, etc.
Ignition includes 900+ SVG icons via the Material Icons Library. Checkout the full set at https://material.io/tools/icons.

entry.key: This example project demonstrates how Ignition can be used to locate employees that move between multiple sites using RFID badges. The demo will periodically update the locations of randomly selected employees. You can also use the button in the menu to trigger a random badge scan.

properties.entry: This example project demonstrates how Ignition can be used to locate employees that move between multiple sites using RFID badges. The demo will periodically update the locations of randomly selected employees. You can also use the button in the menu to trigger a random badge scan.

With all of the alarm data recorded, you can use that data to measure KPIs and create a Summary view. You can also visualize alarms, including the ability to sort them by specific categories, like frequency or duration.

To start storing values, a designer simply needs to drag and drop tags onto a Transaction Group. The example to the right shows a group that pulls form a PLC every 15 seconds. How often data is pulled, how long it's stored and it's destination can all be customized to suit the projects specific needs.
Ignition can easily connect to web services such as weather forecasts as shown below. Web services can also be used to connect to many web-based applications such as ERP systems.

For those who prefer more colorful and realistic visualization, Ignition also supports Traditional HMI techniques. Ignition comes with Symbol Factory, an extensive library of Industrial SVG images for designing real-time status and control screens. Animation is supported and Components can be rotated, moved, or resized dynamically.
See what you can do with Ignition
See how pages are made and added to the navigation in this video.
Download the Perspective app and tour the demo project on your phone. Play around with GPS and barcode scanning. Every page in the demo project is designed to work on phones, tablets, and PCs.

Ignition excels at historizing PLC data into a SQL database, but that same connection can be used for other applications as well. Ignition excels as a front-end to a database application like this simple Inventory Management example.

Perspective is mobile-responsive, so it automatically adapts to fit screens of any size, giving you a personalized view into your unique processes that are automatically optimized for whatever device you're on. To see this in action, try viewing this demo on a variety of devices and see how it automatically adjusts.
Ignition allows you to lock down your application at multiple levels. Ignition has a built-in authentication source or can use third party platforms using SAML and OpenID Connect.
**This is bold text** *This is italic text* ~~Strikethrough~~

Adding the Cirrus Link MQTT Modules for Ignition to the universal Ignition platform empowers companies to set up their own IIoT solution on a secure MQTT Message-Oriented Middleware (MOM) infrastructure. The architecture of Ignition IIoT is flexible: you can set it up in the Cloud, on a private on-premise network, or a hybrid of both.

Ignition has a feature called "retargeting" that provides a way for clients running a particular project to switch to a different project on the fly, even if the other project is hosted on a different Ignition Fateway. Click on the IIot Project aboce to try it out.
As alarms are triggered they can be viewed in the Alarm Status Table where they can be filtered by alarm state. Alarms can be acknowledged directly in the table.
The Alarm Journal Table makes it easy to sort through past alarms and see when the alarms changed state. Also, you can see who acknowledged the alarm and any other defined associated data.

Pages are the main method of navigation in Perspective. A page represents a collection of views that are displayed in a single space. Just like a single tab of a web browser, this represents a single page at a time.

Users want to view the status of alarms from within a client. Fortunately, alarms can be visualized in several ways. By far the most popular way to visualize alarm status is with the Alarm Status Table component.
Use the toggle switch to trigger a single alarm. The slider component below can be used to manually adjust the level of a tag, triggering the Hi/Lo alarms on that tag.

The Perspective Power Chart component can be use in a Perspective project to easily visualize tag history. The Power Chart makes it easy for any user to select multiple tags and display the data in a configurable time series chart.
Images such as PNGs, JPGs, or GIFs can be uploaded to the Image Management tool and used inside of views.

entry.key: You must log in as an operator to click this button.

properties.entry: You must log in as an operator to click this button.
See an audit on this demo project in the table below.
Components are the visual elements that fill up your windows with useful content.
This video shows one example of this being done through Auth0 and Google Authenticator.

High Performance HMI techniques and practices call for designs and displays which help the viewer make the best decision in the shortest amount of time after interacting with the HMI. To accomplish this, High Performance HMIs will often look basic and simplistic. They typically use gray-scale colors rather than the traditional graphics and bright colors for their displays. Conceptually, the High Performance HMI operates under the idea of visually contrasting critical and non-critical states. The power of this design philosophy is when something does go wrong, a High Performance HMI will quickly guide the user to the source of the problem.
As alarms are triggered they can be viewed in the Alarm Status Table where they can be filtered by alarm state. Alarms can be acknowledged directly in the table.
Any View inside Perspective can be used as a template, allowing custom components to easily be created, including this Calendar component.

This demo allows for public access, meaning that it can be navigated without logging in. Certain actions can require elevated access based on user permissions. To demo this, there are two buttons featured below. The first is public, but the second requires the operator role. Test it for yourself by clicking the Sign In button in the header and log in with operator/password.
